title: Decoy Handoff — Read the Slip, Not the Bait
game_context: 5-on-5 half-court. You are on the right wing in triple-threat. Your post (5) is at the elbow and walks into a dribble-handoff at you — but the handoff is a decoy. The scouting report says x1 (on-ball) jumps every DHO. Beat 1 freezes on the matchup. The dummy DHO fires; x1 commits to the handoff (the bait). The screener pivots and slips behind him to the rim. Beat 2 freezes on the slip — the real read.
possession_setup: Right wing triple-threat. 5 walks toward you to set up the DHO at the elbow extended. x1 is shaded hard to the handoff side — your scouting says he will jump it. x5 is in soft drop, expecting to absorb a roll. Weak-side defenders are in shell: x3 in the strong-side corner is shaded slightly toward the gap, x2 sits in the weak-side corner, x4 is one pass away from the weak slot. No tag pre-positioned at the rim.
decision_moment: Beat one freezes on the matchup — observe x1's shading, x5's drop, the empty rim. The play unfreezes; 5 starts the DHO, x1 jumps the handoff (the bait), 5 fakes the handoff and slips behind him to the rim. Beat two freezes on the slip — x1's hand is reaching toward the empty ball-fake, his foot has crossed over and is gone, x5 is a half-step late, x4 has not started his rotation. The lane to the rim is open. The choice tray opens here.
visible_cue: Beat 1: x1 shaded to the handoff side (hip arrow), mismatch pulse on x1, rim corridor visible. Beat 2: x1's hand reaching where the handoff would have been (hand in lane), his recovery foot pointing back toward the handoff side (foot arrow), mismatch pulse retained — the screener has slipped behind him and the lane is open.
prompt: The DHO is bait. Beat 1 — see the matchup. Beat 2 — read the slip. What's the real read?

choices.label: Skip the handoff — slip-pass to the screener at the rim.
choices.feedback_text: Best read. The DHO was the decoy; x1 bit. Your screener slipped behind him before x5 could rotate. That is HUNT D3 — the bait is the first action, the slip is the answer.

choices.label: Take the handoff anyway and attack x5 in drop.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. Taking the handoff means going where the defense expects you. You read the bait, not the slip. The screener is already past x1 with the lane open.

choices.label: Pull the ball back out and reset the action.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. Resetting wastes the trap your screener just sprung. The decoy worked once — the defense's recovery will close the window next possession.

choices.label: Swing the ball weak-side to the wing for the open three.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. x4 has not over-helped — he is still home on the weak wing. That is a SKR read (no rotation to punish) and it skips the slip the defense actually gave you.

best_read: Slip-pass the screener to the rim. The dummy DHO was bait for x1; he bit, and his back foot is gone. Your screener slipped behind him before x5 could close the pocket and before x4 could tag. The rim corridor is open for exactly the moment the choice tray is open — read the second freeze, not the bait.
bad_reads: Take the handoff — going where the defense set the bait undoes the decoy.
Reset — wastes the slip; defense's next coverage will not bite the same way.
Swing weak-side — chases a SKR read against a defense that has not rotated.
common_miss_reason: Reading the bait as the read. Players who learned HUNT D1/D2 know to wait for the second freeze, but D3 introduces a decoy: the first action is the bait, and the read only exists because of the defense's reaction to the bait. Committing to the handoff means the player decided one beat early.
why_best_read_works: A decoy action is a question the defense answers with their body. The dummy DHO asks x1 'will you jump this?' and x1 answers yes — the moment his outside foot crosses over is the moment his recovery angle commits. The slip pass behind him exploits that commitment before x5 can close the rim or x4 can leave his man. The window is small (under 400ms) but unmissable when the read is taken on the second freeze, not the bait.
decoder_teaching_point: HUNT D3 introduces decoy actions. The first action — the handoff, the slip, the dummy screen — is a question, not the answer. Beat 1: see the matchup. Beat 2: read what the defense's reaction to the decoy left open. Take that, not the bait.

feedback.correct: Good chain. You let the DHO be bait, x1 bit, and you took the slip the bite created. That is HUNT D3 — the decoy is the question, the slip is the answer.
feedback.wrong: Read the second freeze. Beat 1 named the matchup; the unfreeze fired the bait; beat 2 was the slip. You stopped at the bait.

self_review_checklist: Did I treat the DHO as a question, not the answer?
Did I wait for beat 2 before committing?
Did I track the screener's slip behind x1, not the ball?
Did I take the rim corridor before x5 could rotate or x4 could tag?

coach_validation.notes: Pack 2 (Phase δ-A) — HUNT-03 base scenario. D3: decoy actions (dummy DHO into screener slip). Two-freeze beatSpec: beat 1 matchup observation, beat 2 the real read after the bait plays out in the inter-beat unfreeze. The cognition module's HUNT_TEMPLATES carries a beat-3 cue/action/advantage triad reused across both freezes per §9.1 cadence reuse. coach_validation.level=high is required at D3+ per LINT-HUNT-05.

source_research_basis: Standard NBA / college decoy-action vocabulary. The dummy DHO into a slip is canonical against ball-hawking on-ball defenders (the scouting line 'x1 jumps the DHO every time' is the exact tell offensive coordinators look for). HUNT D3 cognition — treat the first action as a question, not the answer — is universally taught above varsity high school.
explanation_md: **Hunt the Advantage — Decoy Actions.** A decoy action is a basketball move whose only job is to make the defense move. The handoff is not the play; the handoff is the *question*. Beat one shows you the matchup — your post is in DHO range, x1 is shaded hard to the handoff. The unfreeze fires the bait: the handoff motion starts, x1 jumps it, his outside foot crosses over, and your screener slips behind him to the rim. Beat two freezes on the slip — x1's hand is in the lane where the ball would have been, his recovery foot is gone, x5 is late, x4 has not committed. **Beat one for the matchup. Beat two for the slip. Take the rim before the recovery catches up.**

wrongDemos.caption: You took the handoff. x1 recovered into the lane and x5 was set in the pocket.
You reset. The decoy worked once and you spent it.
You swung weak-side. x4 was already home; x3 closed out clean.
